mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or positive forward returns occur in less than 50% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0%, corporate earnings growth collapses due to reduced consumer spending and labor market slack, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.0% for three consecutive months, or the signal shows zero predictive accuracy in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts signal synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative forward returns in more than 55% of occurrences over a 5-year backtest, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply-side capacity utilization and corporate output growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Short-term returns are negative in more than 65% of months where INDPRO rises, or the signal loses predictive power when inflation exceeds 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a federal funds rate below 0.5% and a consumer price index below 220.0 creates a highly negative real interest rate environment, forcing capital into equities and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to produce positive returns in more than 50% of cases, or real rate compression shows no statistical correlation with equity performance over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding economic activity and rising household income, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict positive returns with a Rank IC above 0.01 over any 24-month rolling window, or the signal direction reverses during inflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in real gross domestic product exceeding 1.0% signals severe aggregate demand destruction, triggering equity market repricing and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of quarters where GDP contracts by more than 1.0%, or the signal shows zero predictive accuracy in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in industrial production and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm a synchronized supply and labor market recovery, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The dual signal produces negative forward returns in more than 60% of triggered months, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression over a 3-year walk-forward test.
